related.value: Phase 3 · pick CRUD + read-side locking · shipped

meta.value: Complete — authored as the approval doc it would have been

blocks.heading: Let players plan their whole season — and let a cron job, not the user, turn plans into real picks.

rows.value: A **Season Planner**: slot golfers into any future tournament as `PLANNED` prepicks; the daily cron promotes valid plans to `LOCKED` in the database when the tournament starts.

PPGL's strategy game is season-long — allocate ~200 golfers across ~40 weeks, no reuse. But picks were week-at-a-time: no way to reserve Scheffler for the Masters in March, or see the whole board at once.
Turns the app from a weekly chore into a **persistent strategic tool** — the full remaining schedule with prepick slots, results behind you, plans ahead of you.
2 PRs · 5 new service methods · 5 new tRPC endpoints · 1 cron auto-lock routine · 1 page + picker flow. **No schema migration** — `Pick` already carries `status` + `lockedAt`.

bigIdea.label: The big idea
bigIdea.line: “A plan costs nothing until the cron makes it real.”
bigIdea.sub: A `PLANNED` pick is a **soft reservation** — invisible to opponents, freely editable, and it does *not* consume the golfer. Only the unattended cron promotion to `LOCKED` burns the golfer, earns money, and shows the pick to the league. If a plan turns out invalid on start day, it goes **stale instead of burning the golfer**: the user gets a $0 week, never a lost asset.

blocks.intro: Two paths write the same `pick` row. The user path (top) creates and edits `PLANNED` rows through the planner page — cheap, reversible, private. The cron path (bottom) runs inside `/api/cron/sync`: when a tournament's start date passes, `startTournaments` flips it `UPCOMING → IN_PROGRESS` and — in the same transaction — `lockPlannedPicksForTournament` validates each plan (golfer in the published field, golfer not already used this season) and promotes the valid ones to `LOCKED`. Invalid plans are skipped, not burned. Everything downstream of `LOCKED` — earnings rollup, opponent visibility — is preserved machinery that never learns `PLANNED` exists.
blocks.title: PLANNED → LOCKED lifecycle
The pieces
blocks.intro: One new page, one new cron routine, and a widened pick service — all writing the same `Pick` row. The signature shape of the whole phase is the **two lives of a pick**:

left.title: PLANNED — soft reservation
left.desc: Invisible to opponents, freely editable, and it does **not** consume the golfer. Change it, delete it, or plan the golfer nowhere else — no cost.

right.title: LOCKED — committed pick
right.desc: Burns the golfer for the season, earns real prize money via the rollup, and is **visible to the league**. Identical whether locked manually or by cron.

anatomy.caption: Every pick row is exactly one of these two states, and `@@unique([leagueMemberId, tournamentId])` guarantees one row per member per tournament — so the planner and the Make Pick page can never diverge.

cards.name: pick service — PLANNED CRUD
cards.purpose: Create, swap, and remove plans with validation: tournament must be in the future, golfer must exist, one pick per tournament.

cards.name: pick repository — season queries
cards.purpose: The planner's data spine: all picks (both statuses) with tournament, golfer, and result data, ordered by start date.

cards.name: pick-lock — auto-lock core
cards.purpose: The cron's promotion routine. Built on `db-core` (no `server-only` guard) so the API route and tests can call it; accepts a transaction executor.

cards.name: cron wiring — startTournaments
cards.purpose: On each sync run: find UPCOMING tournaments whose start date passed, flip to IN_PROGRESS and lock plans in one transaction per tournament.

cards.name: Season Planner page
cards.purpose: Full season timeline — completed weeks collapsed with results, upcoming weeks with prepick slots, stale plans flagged as missed. Tap a row to assign.

cards.name: earnings rollup fix
cards.purpose: Both rollup paths filter `pick.status = 'LOCKED'` so a speculative plan can never inflate standings.

blocks.note: Deep detail — the auto-lock algorithm, the tRPC surface, and the `Pick` schema — is in the [reference drawers](#reference).

blocks.title: Where it fits
blocks.intro: Phase 3 built the pick machinery for the current week; Phase 4 extends it across the whole season without changing what a pick *is*. Everything downstream keeps consuming `LOCKED` picks, unaware that some of them were born as plans.

chain.title: Pick CRUD + locking
chain.desc: One pick per week, `PLANNED`/`LOCKED` statuses, read-side locking, remaining-pool queries, the daily `/api/cron/sync` endpoint.

chain.title: Season Planner
chain.desc: Prepicks across the *whole* season + the cron promotion that makes them real — the strategic layer.

chain.title: Earnings · league views · Phase 10
chain.desc: Rollups and opponent views consume `LOCKED` picks unchanged; Phase 10 polish adds drag-and-drop and picker warnings on top.

stack.label: PLANNED CRUD · plan / updatePlan / deletePlan · allSeasonPicks
lockPlannedPicksForTournament (in startTournaments)
stack.note: the write-side promotion inside the existing cron

stack.label: /api/cron/sync · tournament.schedule · resolveLeagueMemberId
stack.note: existing cron endpoint, schedule query, and member scoping — reused as-is

stack.label: Pick schema + @@unique(leagueMemberId, tournamentId)
stack.note: no migration; the constraint is what makes update-in-place safe

stack.label: earnings rollup machinery
stack.note: only gains a LOCKED filter — the calculation is untouched

blocks.title: Key decisions
blocks.intro: The whole design hangs on the first two: what a plan *reserves*, and *who* turns it into a real pick.

decisions.q: What does a PLANNED pick reserve?
decisions.a: Nothing — it's a soft reservation. Only `LOCKED` picks consume a golfer for the season.
decisions.why: Planning three months out shouldn't cost options today. The exhausted-golfer query counts only `LOCKED` picks, so a golfer can be planned for week 10 *and* picked for week 8.
decisions.rejected: Hard reservation (PLANNED blocks the remaining pool) — one speculative plan would lock away a golfer the user may never actually field, and stale plans would silently shrink the pool.

decisions.q: How does a plan become a real pick?
decisions.a: Write-side: the daily cron promotes valid `PLANNED` → `LOCKED` in the database on tournament start day.
decisions.why: After promotion the pick is *indistinguishable* from a manually submitted one — it burns the golfer, earns via the rollup, and is visible to opponents. One code path downstream, forever.
decisions.rejected: Extending read-side locking (compute LOCKED at read time, never write). Earnings, opponent visibility, and pool queries would each need PLANNED-aware special cases — three subtle forks instead of one DB write.

decisions.a: At lock time (cron), never at plan time.
decisions.why: Tournament fields aren't published until a few days before the event — plan-time validation is impossible for most of the season, so validation moves to the only moment the field is known.
decisions.rejected: Per-tournament "likely in field" predictions in the picker — there's no historical field-entry data to predict from. Deferred; the picker shows the global remaining pool instead.

decisions.q: What happens to a plan that fails validation on start day?
decisions.a: It goes stale — skipped by the cron and flagged "missed" on the timeline. The user gets $0 that week but keeps the golfer.
decisions.why: Burning a golfer the user never actually fielded would punish twice for one mistake. Worst case is a lost week, never a lost asset.

decisions.q: What if Make Pick submits over an existing PLANNED pick?
decisions.a: `submit` updates the existing pick's golfer in place; `LOCKED` picks are rejected with `PRECONDITION_FAILED`.
decisions.why: `@@unique([leagueMemberId, tournamentId])` guarantees one row per member per tournament — both flows converge on that row instead of racing to create duplicates.

decisions.q: What does the planner's season query return?
decisions.a: Raw DB status from `getAllSeasonPicks` — no read-side masking.
decisions.why: The planner must distinguish `PLANNED` (editable), `LOCKED` (done), and stale `PLANNED` (missed). Read-side locking would erase exactly the distinction the page exists to show.

decisions.q: Where does the auto-lock code live?
decisions.a: A `pick-lock` module built on `db-core` — no `server-only` guard — following the `earnings-core` pattern.
decisions.why: The cron API route and tests both need to call it. Its two validation queries are inlined rather than spun into a new repo file — not worth the indirection.

decisions.q: How do users assign a golfer to a week?
decisions.a: Tap-to-assign: tap a tournament row, pick from a searchable, world-ranking-sorted drawer.
decisions.why: Simpler to build and works on mobile, where most picks happen.
decisions.rejected: Drag-and-drop planning — deferred to Phase 10 polish. High interaction cost on touch screens for zero strategic benefit in v1.

blocks.title: Scope
inList: `PLANNED` pick CRUD across repository, service, and tRPC router (`plan`, `updatePlan`, `deletePlan`, `plannedPicks`, `allSeasonPicks`)
`submit` update-in-place semantics for existing `PLANNED` picks; hard reject on `LOCKED`
Cron auto-lock: `UPCOMING → IN_PROGRESS` flip + `PLANNED → LOCKED` promotion, atomic per tournament
Earnings rollup filtered to `status = 'LOCKED'` in both rollup paths
Season Planner page: full timeline, status indicators (planned / locked / missed / results), completed weeks collapsed
Golfer picker: remaining pool, name search, world-ranking sort
NavBar link + responsive layout with brand tokens

outList.text: Drag-and-drop planning — tap-to-assign only in v1
Per-tournament "likely in field" predictions — no historical field-entry data exists
Warning when picking a golfer already LOCKED elsewhere
Manual PLANNED → LOCKED confirmation step — rejected outright; the cron is the confirmation
Auto-deleting stale plans — they stay visible as "missed" so users see what slipped

blocks.title: Rollout
blocks.intro: Backend lands first and is fully test-gated before any UI exists — the cron promotion is unattended, so its three validation outcomes are proven in PR 1, not discovered in production.

phases.title: PLANNED service layer + cron auto-lock
phases.scope: Repository queries (`findAllSeasonPicks`, `findPlannedPicksForTournament`, `findSeasonPlannedGolferIds`), five service methods, `submit` update-in-place semantics, `lockPlannedPicksForTournament` wired into `startTournaments` inside `/api/cron/sync`, `LOCKED` filter on both earnings rollups, and the five router endpoints.
gate: Plan CRUD tests: creation, duplicate-golfer validation, swap, delete, can't plan a started tournament
`submit` updates an existing PLANNED pick in place and rejects LOCKED with PRECONDITION_FAILED
Auto-lock: valid pick → LOCKED · golfer not in field → stays unburned · golfer already used → skipped
Earnings rollup excludes PLANNED picks in both paths

phases.title: Season Planner page
phases.scope: The `/planner` timeline built on `allSeasonPicks` + `tournament.schedule`: per-tournament name/dates/purse/status, pick status indicators, collapsed completed section with results, "Add Plan" → golfer picker (search + world ranking), Change/Remove on planned picks, NavBar link.
gate: A user can plan picks across the full remaining season and see them auto-lock on start day
Stale plans render with a "missed" indicator and can be manually removed
Completed tournaments show results but stay de-emphasized
Responsive layout on mobile with brand tokens

blocks.title: Definition of done
blocks.intro: Done is behavioral: the three auto-lock outcomes, the two `submit` outcomes, and a clean earnings ledger. The movers are what the phase changes about the product.

cells: Auto-lock · golfer in field, not used
the happy path pays out exactly like a manual pick
worst case is a $0 week, never a lost asset
the season-long no-reuse rule holds even for unattended writes
Make Pick and Planner converge on one row
locked means locked — no path rewrites history

blocks.note: All outcomes are covered by the PR 1 test gate; the phase shipped with every checklist item in the source plan checked.

blocks.title: Risks
blocks.intro: The risk profile is concentrated in one place: an unattended cron writing money-bearing rows.

risks.risk: The cron misses or fails the start-day run — plans never lock, users silently get $0 weeks.
risks.mitigation: Catch-up by construction: `startTournaments` selects every `UPCOMING` tournament with `startDate <= now` on *every* run, so the next successful run promotes whatever the failed one missed. Promotion is idempotent — it only touches rows still `PLANNED`.

risks.risk: Partial failure mid-promotion: tournament flipped to IN_PROGRESS but its picks left unlocked.
risks.mitigation: One transaction per tournament: the status flip and `lockPlannedPicksForTournament` share a `withTransaction` scope — either both commit or neither does.

risks.risk: PLANNED rows leak into money or visibility paths and inflate standings or spoil picks.
risks.mitigation: Explicit LOCKED filters in both earnings rollup queries and the league current-week view, each covered by tests — `PLANNED` is invisible everywhere except the owner's planner.

risks.risk: A user edits a plan at the same moment the cron locks it.
risks.mitigation: Start-date guard on every mutation: `updatePlan`/`deletePlan` re-check the tournament and reject with `PRECONDITION_FAILED` once it has started; the cron only touches rows still `PLANNED`. No cross-member race exists — golfer uniqueness is per-member.

risks.risk: Stale plans confuse users — a week quietly earns nothing.
risks.mitigation: Visible, not deleted: stale plans stay on the timeline with a "missed" indicator so users see exactly what slipped, and can clear it themselves.

blocks.title: Before you approve
blocks.intro: This page is the approval surface for the phase as designed; it shipped in exactly this shape (with a few post-ship evolutions logged in the reference).

commit.title: What you're approving
commit.body: A two-PR staged delivery: PR 1 lands the service layer + cron promotion behind a full test gate before any UI exists; PR 2 is additive UI. No schema migration — `Pick` already carries `status`, `lockedAt`, and the unique constraint. The only shared surfaces touched are `submit` and the earnings rollup, both test-gated, so rollback is a straight revert.

scrutinize.title: Scrutinize hardest
scrutinize.body: `lockPlannedPicksForTournament` — an unattended DB write that moves money. Too-strict validation hands out silent $0 weeks; too-loose burns golfers users never fielded or double-locks a golfer. Walk the promotion loop and its two checks before signing off. See the algorithm walkthrough ›

open.title: Still open — non-blocking
open.note: Deferred to polish; none block PR 1 or PR 2.
questions: Should the planner show remaining golfers per world-ranking tier ("3 top-25 left")?
Does the golfer picker need virtualization at 200+ golfers, or is search + ranking sort enough?
Should the picker warn when a golfer is already LOCKED for another week? (deferred)

blocks.title: Reference
blocks.intro: Full depth for the careful reader — the auto-lock algorithm, the API surface, the schema, the open-question log, and how the shipped code evolved past this plan.

items.summary: Auto-lock algorithm — the promotion loop, both checks, and the transaction
tRPC surface — five new endpoints on the pick router
items.markdown:
All endpoints are `protectedProcedure`s scoped through `resolveLeagueMemberId(userId, leagueId)` — a user can only ever touch their own membership's picks.

| Endpoint | Kind | Service | Semantics |
|---|---|---|---|
| `pick.plan` | mutation | `planPick` | Creates a `PLANNED` pick. Rejects: unknown tournament/golfer (`NOT_FOUND`), started tournament (`PRECONDITION_FAILED`), existing pick for the tournament (`CONFLICT`). |
| `pick.updatePlan` | mutation | `updatePlannedPick` | Swaps the golfer on an existing `PLANNED` pick. `NOT_FOUND` unless the pick exists *and* is `PLANNED` — LOCKED picks are unreachable. |
| `pick.deletePlan` | mutation | `removePlannedPick` | Deletes a `PLANNED` pick; same PLANNED-only guard, plus the tournament must not have started. |
| `pick.plannedPicks` | query | `getPlannedPicks` | The member's `PLANNED` picks for the season. |
| `pick.allSeasonPicks` | query | `getAllSeasonPicks` | Every pick (both statuses) with tournament, golfer, purse, and result data — the planner timeline's spine, ordered by start date. |

The Make Pick flow's guard is the same shape: an existing `PLANNED` pick is updated in place; a `LOCKED` pick rejects with `PRECONDITION_FAILED`.

The phase rides entirely on the existing model — `status` defaults to `PLANNED`, `lockedAt` records the promotion moment, and the unique constraint is what makes update-in-place safe:

```prisma
model Pick {
  id             String     @id @default(uuid())
  leagueMemberId String     @map("league_member_id")
  tournamentId   String     @map("tournament_id")
  golferId       String     @map("golfer_id")
  status         PickStatus @default(PLANNED)
  createdAt      DateTime   @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt      DateTime   @updatedAt @map("updated_at")
  lockedAt       DateTime?  @map("locked_at")

  @@unique([leagueMemberId, tournamentId])
  @@map("pick")
}

enum PickStatus {
  PLANNED
  LOCKED
}
```

| # | Question | Resolution |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Should PLANNED picks be visible to opponents? | **No** — only LOCKED picks are visible; auto-locked picks follow the same visibility rules as any other. |
| 2 | Show remaining golfers per ranking tier? | *Deferred to polish.* |
| 3 | How does the picker handle 200+ golfers? | Search + world-ranking sort; *virtualize only if slow.* |
| 4 | Should PLANNED picks block golfer availability? | **No** — soft reservation; only LOCKED consumes. |
| 5 | Validate field membership at plan time? | **No** — fields unknown for future tournaments; validate at auto-lock. |
| 6 | Manual PLANNED → LOCKED confirmation step? | **No** — the daily cron is the confirmation. |
| 7 | Make Pick conflicts with a PLANNED pick? | **`submit` updates the golfer in place; LOCKED rejected.** |
| 8 | What happens to stale PLANNED picks? | **Left in place with a "missed" indicator; not auto-deleted.** |
| 9 | Combined query for all pick statuses? | **Yes** — `getAllSeasonPicks` returns PLANNED + LOCKED. |
| 10 | Does auto-lock need to write to the DB? | **Yes** — promotion burns the golfer, enables earnings, reveals the pick. |
| 11 | Should earnings rollup filter by status? | **Yes** — `WHERE pick.status = 'LOCKED'` in both paths. |

items.summary: As shipped — how the live code evolved past this plan
items.markdown:
The phase shipped as designed, and the PLANNED/LOCKED model then absorbed every later feature **without a schema change** — the best evidence the core decisions held. Verified deltas between this plan and today's code:

- **Cron cadence:** daily 10:00 UTC → **every 15 minutes** (`vercel.json`), so locks land within minutes of a start time rather than hours.
- **Route:** the planner shipped at **`/season`** (NavBar "Season") rather than `/planner`, merged with the season history view.
- **`maxGolferUses`:** the binary "already used" check generalized to a per-league use limit (`findExhaustedGolferIds`), letting leagues allow golfer reuse.
- **Stale-plan handling hardened:** skip-mode now *deletes* a not-in-field plan at lock time (golfer returns to the pool immediately), leagues can opt into **`burnStalePicks`** to lock it anyway, and a post-lock cleanup removes other plans for golfers that just became exhausted.
- **Duplicate-plan UX:** planning a golfer already planned elsewhere now **moves the plan** instead of rejecting it.
- **Pre-lock visibility:** leagues can opt into **`showPicksBeforeLock`** to reveal PLANNED picks to the league early.
- **`planMultiple`:** one tap can plan a golfer across all of a user's leagues, skipping ineligible ones per league.

One reversal worth noting: `getAllSeasonPicks` now *does* apply read-side locking (the plan chose raw status); stale plans are instead surfaced by the timeline's own start-date check and largely removed at lock time by the cron cleanup.
